title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled Recipe - Easy & Authentic
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled with our easy recipe! Get juicy, smoky flavors at home. Perfect for an Indian dinner. Step-by-step guide included.

seo.h1: Authentic Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled Recipe

seo.imageAlt: Close-up of juicy, smoky Tandoori Chicken pieces fresh off the charcoal grill, garnished with cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the authentic taste of India with our Tandoori Chicken, perfectly cooked on a charcoal grill for that irresistible smoky flavor.

description: Experience the fiery flavors of India with our authentic Tandoori Chicken, perfectly cooked over charcoal. This recipe brings the smoky, tender chicken straight from the tandoor to your backyard grill, promising an unforgettable dinner. Each bite is bursting with aromatic spices and a beautiful char, making it a true culinary delight.

ingredients.notes: cut into 8-10 pieces, or use boneless thighs/breasts

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Clean and pat dry the chicken pieces thoroughly. Using a sharp knife, make deep slits on the flesh of each chicken piece. This allows the marinade to penetrate deeply and infuse maximum flavor.

instructions.title: First Marinade Application
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste, 1 tablespoon of lemon juice, 1 teaspoon of salt, and 1/2 teaspoon of Kashmiri red chili powder. Rub this mixture generously over all the chicken pieces, ensuring it gets into the slits. Let the chicken rest for 15-20 minutes at room temperature.

instructions.title: Prepare the Second Marinade
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the thick plain yogurt, the remaining ginger-garlic paste, remaining lemon juice, 1/2 teaspoon salt, remaining red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, and black pepper. If using, add a few drops of red food color for that classic tandoori hue. Stir in the mustard oil until well combined and smooth.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Add the chicken pieces from the first marinade to the second yogurt-spice mixture. Ensure every piece is thoroughly coated. Cover the bowl tightly and refrigerate for a minimum of 4-6 hours, or ideally overnight, to allow the flavors to fully develop and the chicken to tenderize.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: About 30-40 minutes before you plan to cook, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the hot coals to create both direct and indirect heat zones. The grill should reach a medium-high temperature.

instructions.title: Grill the Tandoori Chicken
instructions.text: Thread the marinated chicken pieces onto skewers, shaking off any excess marinade. Place the skewers over the direct heat initially for a few minutes to get a good char, then move to indirect heat. Cook for 25-35 minutes, turning frequently, until the chicken is cooked through, tender, and beautifully charred. Baste with any leftover marinade or a little oil during the last few minutes of cooking.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Once cooked, carefully remove the chicken from the skewers. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro and sprinkle with chaat masala. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges, mint chutney, and sliced onion rings.

tips: For truly tender chicken, marinating overnight is crucial. The acidity in the yogurt helps break down the chicken fibers.
Using Kashmiri red chili powder gives a vibrant red color without making the dish overly spicy, which is characteristic of authentic tandoori chicken.
Baste the chicken with a little melted butter or oil during the last few minutes of grilling to keep it moist and add a lovely sheen.
article: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled is a beloved Indian Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
